description: Civil war between the Union (North) and Confederacy (South) over slavery and states' rights.
significance: Abolished slavery; strengthened federal authority.

description: Thirteen British colonies in North America fought for independence.
significance: Birth of the United States; inspired global independence movements.

description: British Crown took direct control of India after the 1857 Rebellion.
significance: Formalized British colonial rule in India (until 1947).

description: John Cadbury opened a shop selling drinking chocolate, founding the Cadbury company.
significance: Launched one of the world's most iconic confectionery brands.

description: Darwin's voyage aboard HMS Beagle, inspiring his evolutionary theories.
significance: Collected data leading to the theory of evolution.

description: Christopher Columbus's first voyage landed in the Americas (most likely the Bahamas), marking a major turning point in world history.
significance: This event initiated European exploration and colonization in the Americas.

description: Lincoln declared all enslaved people in Confederate states 'forever free'.
significance: Pivotal in abolishing slavery; shifted Civil War's purpose.

eventName: End of World War I (Armistice Day)
description: End of World War I marked a turning point in global politics.
significance: Marked the end of a devastating war and reshaped international relations.

description: William Sturgeon built the first practical electric motor, demonstrating electromagnetism.
significance: Laid groundwork for electric industrialization.

description: Conflicts between China and Britain over opium trade and sovereignty.
significance: Forced China to open ports; began 'Century of Humiliation'.

description: World's first stock exchange established by the Dutch East India Company in Amsterdam.
significance: Created modern stock trading; enabled shareholder capitalism.

description: British East India Company established Singapore as a trading post.
significance: Became a key global trade hub; remains a financial center.

eventName: Founding of the Dutch East India Company
description: Dutch East India Company (VOC) founded, becoming the world's first multinational corporation.
significance: Pioneered modern corporate structure and global trade.

description: Overthrow of the French monarchy, leading to republicanism and Napoleon's rise.
significance: Spread democratic ideals; destabilized European monarchies.

description: The Great Exhibition showcased global industrial innovations in London's Crystal Palace.
significance: First World's Fair; symbolized British industrial dominance.

description: Transition to industrialized economies, starting in Britain with mechanized production and steam power.
significance: Transformed global economies; spurred urbanization and imperialism.

description: Montgolfier brothers launched the first manned hot-air balloon flight in Paris.
significance: Marked the birth of human flight; inspired aviation development.

description: John Harrison invented the marine chronometer, solving longitude measurement at sea.
significance: Revolutionized naval navigation; enabled precise global exploration.

description: Extreme weather and crop failures caused mass starvation in Ireland.
significance: Killed 300,000+; led to early Irish emigration.

description: Victor Hugo published his epic novel about social injustice in France.
significance: Classic of world literature; influenced social reforms.

description: World's first underground railway opened between Paddington and Farringdon.
significance: Pioneered urban rapid transit; model for subway systems.

description: James Clerk Maxwell formulated equations unifying electricity and magnetism.
significance: Laid foundation for modern physics (e.g., radio, light waves).

description: Overthrow of the Tokugawa shogunate; modernization of Japan under Emperor Meiji.
significance: Japan industrialized rapidly; became a global power.

description: Napoleon Bonaparte crowned himself Emperor, consolidating power in France.
significance: Marked peak of French imperial ambitions; spread Napoleonic Code.

description: Napoleon Bonaparte's campaigns to dominate Europe, countered by coalitions.
significance: Reshaped European borders; spread nationalism.

description: New Zealand became a British colony with the signing of the Treaty of Waitangi.
significance: Established British sovereignty; began Maori land dispossession.

description: New York Stock & Exchange Board founded on Wall Street.
significance: Became the world's largest stock exchange; symbol of American capitalism.

eventName: On the Origin of Species Published
description: Charles Darwin published his theory of evolution by natural selection.
significance: Revolutionized biology; foundational for modern genetics.

description: U.S. Navy forced Japan to open trade after 200+ years of isolation.
significance: Started Japan's modernization (Meiji Restoration).

description: Mary Shelley published the first science fiction novel, 'Frankenstein; or, The Modern Prometheus'.
significance: Founded the sci-fi genre; explored ethics of scientific creation.

description: Anti-colonial uprisings in Upper and Lower Canada against British rule.
significance: Spurred democratic reforms; led to Canadian Confederation debates.

description: European powers partitioned Africa into colonies during the Berlin Conference.
significance: Exploited African resources; imposed unstable borders.

description: Second conflict expanding foreign control over Chinese trade and territory.
significance: Further weakened Qing dynasty; legalized opium trade.

description: Global war involving Britain, France, Prussia, Austria, and others, fought in Europe, Americas, and Asia.
significance: Britain became the dominant colonial power; France lost Canada.

description: British Parliament abolished slavery in most of the British Empire.
significance: Freed 800,000+ enslaved people; compensated slave owners.

description: Artificial waterway connecting the Mediterranean and Red Sea, boosting global trade.
significance: Reduced travel time between Europe and Asia; enhanced trade.

description: Massive civil war in China led by Hong Xiuquan against the Qing dynasty.
significance: 20-30 million deaths; bloodiest conflict of the 19th century.

description: Samuel Morse patented the electric telegraph, revolutionizing long-distance communication.
significance: Enabled instant communication; precursor to modern telecom.

eventName: The Raft of the Medusa Painted
description: Théodore Géricault completed his monumental painting depicting a shipwreck scandal.
significance: Pioneered Romanticism; critiqued government incompetence.

description: Treaty ending the First Opium War; ceded Hong Kong to Britain.
significance: Marked start of British colonial rule in Hong Kong (until 1997).

description: Conflict between the U.S. and Britain over trade restrictions and territorial disputes.
significance: Confirmed U.S. independence; inspired 'The Star-Spangled Banner'.

description: Orville and Wilbur Wright achieved the first sustained powered airplane flight at Kitty Hawk, North Carolina.
significance: Launched the aviation age; transformed travel and warfare within decades.

description: Albert Einstein presented his general theory of relativity to the Prussian Academy of Sciences, describing gravity as the curvature of spacetime.
significance: Revolutionised physics and cosmology; foundation for GPS technology
column8: black hole research and gravitational wave detection.

description: Global war centred in Europe triggered by the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand, involving all major world powers.
significance: 17 million deaths; reshaped borders; led directly to World War II.

description: Two revolutions overthrew the Tsar and brought the Bolsheviks to power under Lenin, creating the Soviet Union.
significance: First communist state; shaped the Cold War and 20th-century geopolitics.

description: Global influenza pandemic infected 500 million people — about a third of the world's population.
significance: 50—100 million deaths; deadliest pandemic in modern history.

eventName: Women's Suffrage in the United States
description: The 19th Amendment granted American women the right to vote, after decades of campaigning.
significance: Landmark in gender equality; inspired suffrage movements worldwide.

description: Armed conflict between the Chinese Nationalist Party (Kuomintang) and the Chinese Communist Party, ending in Communist victory.
significance: Established the People's Republic of China; divided China and Taiwan; reshaped Asia's Cold War alignment.

description: Global economic collapse triggered by the Wall Street Crash, causing mass unemployment and poverty worldwide.
significance: 25% unemployment in US; reshaped economic policy and fuelled the rise of fascism.

description: Adolf Hitler appointed Chancellor of Germany, beginning the Nazi dictatorship and path to World War II.
significance: Led to World War II and the Holocaust; redefined international law and human rights.

description: Global war involving the Allied and Axis powers, fought across Europe, Asia, Africa and the Pacific.
significance: 70—85 million deaths; Holocaust; nuclear bombs; birth of the UN and Cold War.

description: Systematic genocide by Nazi Germany of six million Jews and millions of others across occupied Europe.
significance: Largest genocide of the 20th century; led to creation of Israel and modern human rights law.

description: ENIAC, the first general-purpose electronic computer, was completed at the University of Pennsylvania.
significance: Launched the computer age; foundation for the digital revolution.

description: The United States dropped the first atomic bomb, "Little Boy", on the Japanese city of Hiroshima, killing 70,000—80,000 people instantly.
significance: First use of a nuclear weapon in war; forced Japanese surrender negotiations to begin.

description: The United States dropped the second atomic bomb, "Fat Man", on Nagasaki, Japan, killing 40,000 people instantly.
significance: Second and last use of nuclear weapons in war; Japan surrendered six days later, ending World War II.

description: 51 nations signed the UN Charter in San Francisco, establishing the United Nations to maintain international peace.
significance: First effective global peacekeeping body; framework for international law and cooperation.

description: Decades-long geopolitical tension between the US-led Western bloc and Soviet-led Eastern bloc, fought through proxy wars and an arms race.
significance: Defined world politics for 45 years; nuclear arms race; dozens of proxy conflicts worldwide.

description: India gained independence from British colonial rule, accompanied by partition into India and Pakistan.
significance: End of British Empire in South Asia; partition caused 1—2 million deaths and mass displacement.

description: The State of Israel was proclaimed, leading immediately to war with neighbouring Arab states.
significance: Established the modern Israeli state; central cause of ongoing Middle East conflict.

description: South Africa's institutionalised system of racial segregation and white-minority rule, enforced by law from 1948 until democratic elections in 1994.
significance: Decades of oppression; global anti-apartheid movement; peaceful transition to democracy under Mandela.

eventName: Proclamation of the People's Republic of China
description: Mao Zedong proclaimed the founding of the People's Republic of China in Beijing, following Communist victory in the Civil War.
significance: World's most populous nation became communist; shaped Cold War geopolitics and the 20th-century world order.

description: War between North Korea (backed by China and USSR) and South Korea (backed by UN forces led by the US).
significance: 3 million deaths; Korea remains divided; defined Cold War proxy conflicts.

description: Watson and Crick described the double-helix structure of DNA, the molecule carrying genetic information.
significance: Foundation of modern genetics; led to the Human Genome Project and gene therapy.

description: Prolonged conflict between communist North Vietnam and US-backed South Vietnam; the US withdrew in 1973.
significance: 58,000 US deaths; 2—3 million Vietnamese deaths; reshaped US foreign policy.

description: The Soviet Union launched Sputnik 1, the world's first artificial satellite, into orbit.
significance: Launched the Space Race; led directly to NASA and modern satellite technology.

eventName: First Human in Space — Yuri Gagarin
description: Soviet cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin became the first human to travel into outer space aboard Vostok 1.
significance: Opened the Space Age; accelerated the Space Race between US and USSR.

description: 13-day standoff between the US and USSR over Soviet nuclear missiles in Cuba — closest the Cold War came to nuclear war.
significance: Closest moment to nuclear war in history; led to hotline between US and Soviet leaders.

description: US President John F. Kennedy was assassinated in Dallas, Texas, shocking the world.
significance: Traumatic moment in US history; accelerated civil rights debate and Cold War tensions.

description: US Congress passed the Civil Rights Act, outlawing discrimination based on race, colour, religion, sex or national origin.
significance: Landmark in racial equality; cornerstone of modern civil rights law.

description: Mao Zedong launched a radical political campaign to purge capitalist and traditional elements from Chinese society.
significance: 1—2 million deaths; devastated Chinese culture and education; isolated China internationally.

eventName: Assassination of Martin Luther King Jr.
description: Civil rights leader Martin Luther King Jr. was assassinated in Memphis, Tennessee.
significance: Galvanised the civil rights movement; led to significant legislative and social change.

description: Apollo 11 astronauts Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin became the first humans to walk on the Moon.
significance: Defining achievement of the Space Age; watched live by 600 million people worldwide; "one giant leap for mankind."

description: HIV/AIDS was first identified in the US; the epidemic spread globally, killing tens of millions.
significance: 36+ million deaths; transformed sexual health policy; stigmatised communities worldwide.

description: Explosion at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant in Soviet Ukraine released massive radiation across Europe.
significance: Worst nuclear disaster in history; accelerated the fall of the USSR; reshaped nuclear policy.

description: East Germans began demolishing the Berlin Wall, symbolising the collapse of communist rule in Eastern Europe.
significance: Ended the Cold War division of Germany and Europe; led to German reunification.

description: Tim Berners-Lee published the first website, launching the public World Wide Web.
significance: Transformed communication, commerce and society; foundation of the modern internet.

description: The USSR officially dissolved into 15 independent states, ending the Cold War.
significance: End of the Cold War; reshaped global geopolitics; created 15 new nations.

description: Hutu extremists massacred an estimated 800,000 Tutsi people in 100 days in Rwanda.
significance: Worst genocide since the Holocaust; exposed failures of international response.

description: Nelson Mandela became South Africa's first Black president after the country's first fully democratic election, ending apartheid.
significance: Symbol of peaceful transition and reconciliation; icon of the global struggle against racial injustice.

description: Al-Qaeda terrorists hijacked four aircraft, killing nearly 3,000 people in New York, Washington and Pennsylvania.
significance: Triggered the War on Terror; reshaped global security policy and international relations.

description: Scientists completed the first full sequencing of the human genome, mapping all 3 billion base pairs of human DNA.
significance: Opened era of genomic medicine; transformed biology and medical research.

description: Collapse of Lehman Brothers triggered a global financial crisis — the worst since the Great Depression.
significance: Caused the Great Recession; reshaped global banking regulation; fuelled political populism.

description: The 1920s saw jazz music explode in popularity across America, centred in Harlem, New York, giving rise to a golden era of African-American music, art and literature.
significance: Defined modern American music; gave Black artists global platforms for the first time.

eventName: First Talking Film — The Jazz Singer
description: The Jazz Singer became the first feature-length film with synchronised speech, revolutionising the movie industry.
significance: Ended the silent film era; launched the global Hollywood studio system.

description: Rock and roll music emerged in the United States in the mid-1950s, blending rhythm and blues with country music. Artists like Elvis Presley and Chuck Berry defined the genre.
significance: Transformed popular music worldwide; became the defining cultural movement of the 20th century.

description: The Beatles, a British rock band from Liverpool, became the most influential music group in history, transforming popular culture across the globe.
significance: Shaped fashion, politics and music for generations; sparked the British Invasion of American pop culture.

description: Half a million people gathered on a farm in New York for a music festival that became the defining cultural event of the 1960s counterculture movement.
significance: Symbol of the peace and love generation; captured the spirit of 1960s youth culture worldwide.

description: Norwegian explorer Roald Amundsen led the first expedition to reach the South Pole, beating British explorer Robert Falcon Scott by five weeks.
significance: Last great feat of geographical exploration; Scott's team perished on the return journey.

description: New Zealander Edmund Hillary and Nepali Sherpa Tenzing Norgay became the first people confirmed to have reached the summit of Mount Everest.
significance: Greatest mountaineering achievement; announced on the day of Queen Elizabeth II's coronation.

description: NASA launched Voyager 1, which flew past Jupiter and Saturn before becoming the first human-made object to enter interstellar space in 2012.
significance: Humanity's furthest-reaching object; sent back first close-up images of the outer planets.

description: NASA launched the Hubble Space Telescope into orbit, providing the clearest images of deep space ever captured.
significance: Transformed astronomy; images of distant galaxies changed our understanding of the universe's age and size.

description: NASA's Curiosity rover successfully landed on Mars, beginning a mission to assess whether the planet could have supported microbial life.
significance: Most sophisticated Mars mission to date; found evidence of ancient water and potential habitability.

description: Scottish bacteriologist Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin, the world's first antibiotic, by observing that mould killed bacteria in a petri dish.
significance: Saved hundreds of millions of lives; launched the antibiotic era and modern medicine.

description: Jonas Salk announced the first successful polio vaccine, effectively ending one of the most feared childhood diseases in the world.
significance: Near-eradicated polio globally; one of the greatest public health achievements in history.

description: The first message was sent over ARPANET, the US military network that became the foundation of the modern internet.
significance: Direct predecessor of the internet; transformed global communication, commerce and society.

description: Martin Cooper of Motorola made the first handheld mobile phone call in New York City.
significance: Launched the mobile revolution; by 2020 there were more mobile phones than people on Earth.

eventName: Dolly the Sheep — First Cloned Mammal
description: Dolly the sheep became the first mammal to be successfully cloned from an adult cell, at the Roslin Institute in Scotland.
significance: Proved adult cell cloning was possible; sparked global debate about genetic ethics.

description: Apple launched the first iPhone, combining a phone, music player and internet browser into a single touchscreen device.
significance: Launched the smartphone era; transformed communication, navigation, photography and daily life.

description: OpenAI launched ChatGPT, a conversational AI assistant that reached 100 million users in two months — the fastest-growing application in history.
significance: Triggered a global AI arms race; began reshaping work, education, creativity and society.

description: The RMS Titanic, the world's largest ocean liner, struck an iceberg on its maiden voyage and sank in the North Atlantic, killing over 1,500 people.
significance: Deadliest peacetime maritime disaster; transformed maritime safety laws internationally.

description: A magnitude 9.1 undersea earthquake off Sumatra triggered a series of massive tsunamis across the Indian Ocean, killing 228,000 people.
significance: Deadliest tsunami in recorded history; triggered the largest international aid response ever seen.

description: A novel coronavirus (SARS-CoV-2) emerged in Wuhan, China and spread globally, causing the worst pandemic since the Spanish Flu. Over 7 million deaths were recorded.
significance: Killed 7+ million people; disrupted the global economy; accelerated remote work and vaccine technology.

description: The Ottoman government systematically massacred and deported 600,000–1.5 million Armenians during and after World War I.
significance: One of the first genocides of the 20th century; shaped modern international law on crimes against humanity.

description: A military coup against Spain's elected government sparked a brutal civil war, with Nazi Germany and Fascist Italy supporting Franco's Nationalists.
significance: Testing ground for WWII weapons and tactics; Franco's dictatorship lasted until 1975.

description: A US-led coalition expelled Iraq from Kuwait after Saddam Hussein's invasion, in the first major conflict of the post-Cold War era.
significance: First televised war in real time; established US military dominance; left unresolved tensions leading to the 2003 Iraq War.

description: Following the September 11 attacks, the US and NATO invaded Afghanistan to dismantle Al-Qaeda and remove the Taliban. The Taliban retook power in 2021.
significance: Longest war in US history; 240,000+ deaths; ended with Taliban returning to power.

description: Russia launched a full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the largest military attack in Europe since World War II.
significance: Caused Europe's largest refugee crisis since WWII; reshaped NATO, global energy markets and the world order.

description: Mahatma Gandhi led a 240-mile march to the sea to protest British salt taxes, galvanising India's independence movement through nonviolent civil disobedience.
significance: Pivotal act of nonviolent resistance; inspired civil rights movements worldwide.

description: East Germany built a concrete wall dividing Berlin overnight, sealing the border between communist East and democratic West Germany.
significance: Physical symbol of the Cold War Iron Curtain; its fall in 1989 signalled the end of the Cold War.

description: Students and citizens gathered in Beijing's Tiananmen Square demanding democratic reforms. The protests were crushed by the Chinese military, with hundreds to thousands killed.
significance: Defining moment of Chinese political history; the iconic 'Tank Man' image became a symbol of resistance.

description: A landmark peace deal ending 30 years of conflict in Northern Ireland, signed by the British and Irish governments and most Northern Irish political parties.
significance: Ended The Troubles, which killed 3,500 people; established power-sharing government in Northern Ireland.

description: Barack Obama was elected the 44th President of the United States, becoming the first African-American to hold the office.
significance: Historic milestone in US racial history; elected amid the Global Financial Crisis.

description: A wave of pro-democracy protests swept across the Arab world, toppling governments in Tunisia, Egypt, Libya and Yemen, and triggering civil war in Syria.
significance: Reshaped the Middle East; social media played a key role; led to both democratic gains and devastating conflicts.

description: The United Kingdom officially left the European Union, following a 2016 referendum in which 52% of British voters chose to leave.
significance: First country to leave the EU; reshaped UK-Europe relations, trade and identity.

description: Arab OPEC members declared an oil embargo against Western nations supporting Israel, causing oil prices to quadruple and triggering a global recession.
significance: Ended the post-WWII economic boom; accelerated interest in renewable energy and energy independence.

description: Eleven European Union countries adopted the euro as a common currency, the largest monetary union in history.
significance: Eliminated currency exchange within Europe; strengthened European economic integration.

eventName: Rise of China as Economic Power
description: China joined the World Trade Organisation, formally integrating into the global economy and beginning two decades of extraordinary economic growth.
significance: China became the world's second-largest economy; lifted 800 million people out of poverty.
